introSection:
All American Triple Loop is a steel roller coaster located at Indiana Beach in Monticello, Indiana, USA. Built by Schwarzkopf, it was the first roller coaster with three vertical loops when it was originally manufactured in 1984.

Before arriving in Indiana, the ride operated on the German fair circuit, then at Sunway Lagoon in Malaysia, then at Flamingo Land in the United Kingdom and then at La Feria Chapultepec Magico in Mexico.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 1984, the ride was purchased by German showman Rudolf Barth. Barth operated the ride as Dreier Looping (German for Three Looping) for twelve years on the German fair circuit. It was the first ride in the world to feature three vertical loops. The ride then arrived at Sunway Lagoon in Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia. It operated from 1997 to 1999 as the Triple Loop Coaster and was painted entirely pink.

In June 1999, the ride was purchased by Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It reopened there in 2000 as Magnum Force. The bottom of the ride was painted red and the top white. It is believed the ride was planned to be called "Mach 3". The ride was sponsored by Wall's Magnum ice cream. It operated until 2005, when after being on sale since August 2004, Magnum Force closed as part of a regeneration of Flamingo Land.

The ride reopened in 2007 at its third location, La Feria Chapultepec Magico in Mexico City, Mexico after a renovation. It was painted yellow with white supports. It was called Montaña Infinitum until 2014 or earlier. It was subsequently renamed Montaña Triple Loop, and later to Quimera, with the loops being repainted red. The coaster had a major accident on September 28, 2019 where the back car derailed, killing two people and injuring two more. The entire park was shut down following the accident.

In November 2020, it was confirmed that Quimera would be sold and relocated to Indiana Beach in Monticello, Indiana, USA. The ride arrived at the park during early 2021. It was overhauled and refurbished, with construction being completed in early 2022. In 2023, trains from the defunct Mindbender at Galaxyland were purchased for use on the ride. The ride opened on May 11, 2024 as All American Triple Loop.

In 2025 or earlier, the ride ran with one of its three cars oriented in reverse.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The roller coaster has a curved lift hill and a tightly curved first drop which is immediately followed by a sharp twisting ascent. Two back-to-back vertical loops follow. The third loop is taken later on.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: The coaster used to be painted red and white, with all supports being white. This later changed to yellow track with white supports and blue loops in Mexico. The loops were later repainted red as Quimera. Before the reopening as All American Triple Loop, the track was repainted blue, the structure white, and the support structure of the loops red.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride was built with 5 trailered trains with 5 cars each. It only operated with 3 trains in Mexico, and the 3 remaining original trains were replaced by a single 3 car non-trailered train from Mindbender at Galaxyland before reopening as All American Triple Loop.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content:
* On Tuesday June 1, 2004, while the ride was operating at Flamingo Land, a 32-year old ride operator was seriously injured when he was struck by a train while taking a shortcut through a restricted area. The man was treated on the scene then airlifted to hospital.


 * On Saturday September 28, 2019, two people were killed and another two injured after the final car derailed while the ride was operating at La Feria Chapultepec Magico.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Montaña Infinitum on Theme Park Review

fullText:
All American Triple Loop is a steel roller coaster located at Indiana Beach in Monticello, Indiana, USA. Built by Schwarzkopf, it was the first roller coaster with three vertical loops when it was originally manufactured in 1984.

Before arriving in Indiana, the ride operated on the German fair circuit, then at Sunway Lagoon in Malaysia, then at Flamingo Land in the United Kingdom and then at La Feria Chapultepec Magico in Mexico.

In 1984, the ride was purchased by German showman Rudolf Barth. Barth operated the ride as Dreier Looping (German for Three Looping) for twelve years on the German fair circuit. It was the first ride in the world to feature three vertical loops. The ride then arrived at Sunway Lagoon in Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia. It operated from 1997 to 1999 as the Triple Loop Coaster and was painted entirely pink.

In June 1999, the ride was purchased by Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It reopened there in 2000 as Magnum Force. The bottom of the ride was painted red and the top white. It is believed the ride was planned to be called "Mach 3". The ride was sponsored by Wall's Magnum ice cream. It operated until 2005, when after being on sale since August 2004, Magnum Force closed as part of a regeneration of Flamingo Land.

The ride reopened in 2007 at its third location, La Feria Chapultepec Magico in Mexico City, Mexico after a renovation. It was painted yellow with white supports. It was called Montaña Infinitum until 2014 or earlier. It was subsequently renamed Montaña Triple Loop, and later to Quimera, with the loops being repainted red. The coaster had a major accident on September 28, 2019 where the back car derailed, killing two people and injuring two more. The entire park was shut down following the accident.

In November 2020, it was confirmed that Quimera would be sold and relocated to Indiana Beach in Monticello, Indiana, USA. The ride arrived at the park during early 2021. It was overhauled and refurbished, with construction being completed in early 2022. In 2023, trains from the defunct Mindbender at Galaxyland were purchased for use on the ride. The ride opened on May 11, 2024 as All American Triple Loop.

In 2025 or earlier, the ride ran with one of its three cars oriented in reverse.

The roller coaster has a curved lift hill and a tightly curved first drop which is immediately followed by a sharp twisting ascent. Two back-to-back vertical loops follow. The third loop is taken later on.

The coaster used to be painted red and white, with all supports being white. This later changed to yellow track with white supports and blue loops in Mexico. The loops were later repainted red as Quimera. Before the reopening as All American Triple Loop, the track was repainted blue, the structure white, and the support structure of the loops red.

The ride was built with 5 trailered trains with 5 cars each. It only operated with 3 trains in Mexico, and the 3 remaining original trains were replaced by a single 3 car non-trailered train from Mindbender at Galaxyland before reopening as All American Triple Loop.


 * On Tuesday June 1, 2004, while the ride was operating at Flamingo Land, a 32-year old ride operator was seriously injured when he was struck by a train while taking a shortcut through a restricted area. The man was treated on the scene then airlifted to hospital.


 * On Saturday September 28, 2019, two people were killed and another two injured after the final car derailed while the ride was operating at La Feria Chapultepec Magico.








 * Montaña Infinitum on Theme Park Review
